entries.title: Doctoral Researcher
bullets: PhD Student with the Predictive Systems in Biomedicine (PRISM) Lab at the Tissue Image Analytics (TIA) Centre.

entries.title: Visiting Researcher - Machine Learning (Object Detection)
bullets: Coauthored a manuscript on depression-like behaviour in zebrafish using a pipeline to analyze 10 hours of data.
Conducted statistical analyses to uncover context-specific advantages of behaviours typically perceived as unfavourable.

entries.title: Research Assistant - Generative AI
bullets: Worked on methods for text-conditioned oral cancer image synthesis.
Leveraged cross-attention-based concept learning to improve embeddings.
Used textual inversion, latent diffusion, and multi-concept-prompt-learning to improve diffusion model performance.

entries.title: Research Assistant - Generative AI
bullets: Advised by Prof. Debayan Gupta.
Enhanced a text-conditioned latent diffusion model, improving FID from 22.39 to 21.11 with 7% lower GPU memory usage.
Conducted literature review and authored a pre-print manuscript under review.

entries.title: Teaching Assistant
bullets: Assisted in teaching CS1206 - Design and Analysis of Algorithms.
Focused on dynamic programming and competitive coding style problems.
Graded assignments, quizzes, and exams, and streamlined discussions for effective learning.

entries.title: Teaching Assistant
bullets: Assisted in teaching CSL2010 - Introduction to Machine Learning.
Graded assignments, quizzes, and exams; facilitated discussions.

entries.title: Teaching Assistant
bullets: Assisted in teaching CS2378 - The New Geography of the Information Age.
Supported development of technical student projects.
Graded assignments, quizzes, and exams; facilitated classroom discussions.

entries.title: Teaching Assistant
bullets: Assisted in teaching CS1230 - Data Structures 1.
Focused on tree structures and dynamic programming.
Graded assignments/exams and held tutorials and additional lectures.

entries.title: Teaching Assistant
bullets: Assisted in teaching CS2378 - The New Geography of the Information Age.
Focused on student research ideas related to surveillance and technology.
Graded assignments, quizzes, and exams; facilitated effective discussions.
